statement: Bennett examined body at 9:40 a.m. and thought death occurred around 2 a.m.
limitations: Secondary within inquest remarks; exact basis in Bennett testimony p.93.

statement: Death could have occurred up to eight hours before his 9:40 a.m. examination, not more than eight hours.
time_estimate: No earlier than about 1:40 a.m.; Bennett put earliest at 2:00 a.m.
limitations: Bennett did not record extent of rigor at the time and described examination as cursory.

statement: Food had been in the stomach for up to 3 or 4 hours before death, with anxiety caveat.
time_estimate: Final meal up to 3-4 hours before death, per Dwyer estimate
limitations: Not a precise time-of-death estimate; digestion variable.

statement: Poison, if involved, must have been taken a few hours before death.
basis: Medical inference from pathology and poison discussion
limitations: Conditional on poison involvement; no agent detected.

statement: If death occurred seven hours after the 7 p.m. sighting, a massive dose would be implied for the suspected drug group.
time_estimate: Conditional seven-hour interval from 7 p.m. movement to about 2 a.m.
limitations: Depends on unproved identity of the man seen at 7 p.m. and unproved poison.

statement: If body had been brought there, doubts about time of death would change/remove some difficulties.
limitations: No evidence here that body was moved; recorded only as caveat.

statement: Estimated probable death at or before midnight in one passage, while other evidence supported around 2 a.m.
time_estimate: At or before midnight per Cleland estimate
limitations: Conflicts with Bennett; not adopted as master chronology event.
